stories.title: Buckley Cemetery gets Flintshire’s first above-ground memorial structure for cremated remains
stories.summary: Flintshire County Council has completed the installation of the county’s first columbarium, a structure providing above-ground storage niches for cremated remains, at Buckley Cemetery. The granite structure has 36 individual niches, each able to hold two sets of cremated remains. Families can choose a personalised inscription on the plaque at the front of each niche.

stories.title: Toyota Deeside Engine Plant charity fund backs St John Ambulance Cymru for second year
stories.summary: St John Ambulance Cymru is to receive £3,000 from the Toyota Manufacturing UK Charitable Trust to fund equipment for volunteers operating across Deeside and North Wales. stories.title: Bristol men charged after Chester theft offences remanded to Crown Court
stories.summary: Two men have been charged with multiple theft offences in Chester following incidents on Wednesday 15 April. stories.title: NSPCC Cymru seeks 12 to 15-year-olds in Wales to join online safety panel
stories.summary: Young people aged 12 to 15 in Wales are being invited to apply to join an NSPCC panel that advises politicians, regulators and tech companies on online safety. NSPCC Cymru is recruiting for the charity’s Voice of Online Youth group, which campaigns to make the internet safer for children. Up to 20 places are available

stories.title: Free Thursday sessions in Shotton and Buckley funded by £48,000 UK Government grant
stories.summary: A free weekly darts club in Shotton and a 60+ exercise programme at a Buckley care home are among the activities running under a county-wide scheme funded by the UK Government Shared Prosperity Fund.
